solution.p: We will factor 3r^{2}-8r+4 where a=3, b=-8, and c=4.
AC Method: In this method, we first calculate the product of a and c in ax^2+bx+c. For this problem, we have (3) \cdot (4) = 12.

The number 12 can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways:  egin{array}{lll}1(12)\amp -1(-12)\amp 2(6)\amp -2(-6)
ewline3(4)\amp -3(-4)\amp \end{array} Looking at the corresponding sums, we want a sum of -8, and this sum happens with (-6)+(-2)=-8 So we write  egin{aligned}[t]3r^{2}-8r+4\amp =3r^{2}-6r-2r+4
ewline \amp =3r\mathopen{}\left(r-2ight)-2\mathopen{}\left(r-2ight)
ewline \amp =\left(3r-2ight)\mathopen{}\left(r-2ight)\end{aligned}Generic Rectangles Method: First, we set up generic rectangles by putting the first term and third term in 3r^{2}-8r+4 into the top left and bottom right rectangles:
The first term's coefficient, 3, can be factored into the product of two numbers (where the first factor is positive) in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(3)\amp \end{array}
The third term, 4, can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(4)\amp -1(-4)\amp 2(2)\amp -2(-2)\newline\end{array}

We put each pair into the corresponding places next to those generic rectangles, and try to match the area of those rectangles with 3r^{2}-8r+4 by guess-and-check. The following generic rectangles show the solution:
Dimensions of those generic rectangles in the diagram give us the solution: \begin{aligned}[t]3r^{2}-8r+4\amp =\left(3r-2\right)\mathopen{}\left(r-2\right)\end{aligned}
